column1: # When an imported xlsx cell has a fill but no explicit font color we
# default to the terminal's own foreground (typically a light color on
# a dark background) so filled and unfilled cells share one aesthetic.
# The exception: light fills like yellow would wash out a light fg, so
# we override to black there.  Mirrors Excel's "automatic" font color
# flipping to black on light fills in a dark theme.
#   B1 'auto-dark'     fill = #C00000 (dark red) no font color
# Pointer starts on A1 — its fill+fg are suppressed under the REVERSED
# highlight, same rule as xlsx_fill / xlsx_font.  Drive past every
# cell under test so the renderer paints them normally.
# Light fill (yellow) — terminal default fg would wash out, override to
# Dark fill (deep red) — terminal default fg reads fine, leave fg unset
# so the cell looks consistent with the surrounding non-filled rows.
# Explicit font color wins even when fill is set.
# Explicit black is collapsed into the "automatic" path (pure black is
# the OOXML automatic-color sentinel — Excel emits `<color theme="1"/>`
# for cells the user never colored, and IronCalc resolves that to
# `Some("#000000")`, indistinguishable from a literal black choice).
# With no fill there's nothing to auto-contrast against, so the cell
# falls back to the terminal's own foreground.
